[1] Ying-Ke Lei, Zhu-Hong You, Zhen Ji, Lin Zhu, and De-Shuang Huang. Assessing and predicting protein interactions by combining manifold embedding with multiple information integration. BMC bioinformatics, 13(Suppl 7):S3, 2012. [ bib ]
[2] J. D. Barrow. The Constants of Nature, chapter New Dimensions, pages 203-205. Random House, New York, 2002. [ bib ]
[3] Immanuel Kant. In Eric Watkins, editor, Kant: Natural Science, chapter Thoughts on the true estimation of living forces, pages 26-28. Cambridge Books, 1st ed. edition, 2012. http://dx.doi.org/10.1017/CBO9781139014380.004. [ bib ]
[4] Paul Ehrenfest. In what way does it become manifest in the fundamental laws of physics that space has three dimensions. Proc. Royal Netherlands Acad. Arts Sci, 20:200-209, 1917. [ bib ]
[5] Greg Egan. Diaspora. Hachette UK, 2010. [ bib ]
[6] Charu C Aggarwal, Alexander Hinneburg, and Daniel A Keim. On the surprising behavior of distance metrics in high dimensional space. Springer, 2001. [ bib ]
[7] Kevin Beyer, Jonathan Goldstein, Raghu Ramakrishnan, and Uri Shaft. When is “nearest neighbor” meaningful? In Database Theory—ICDT’99, pages 217-235. Springer, 1999. [ bib ]
[8] Charu C Aggarwal. Re-designing distance functions and distance-based applications for high dimensional data. ACM SIGMOD Record, 30(1):13-18, 2001. [ bib ]
[9] Nuno Vasconcelos. ECE271B Lecture: Dimensionality and dimensionality reduction. http://www.svcl.ucsd.edu/courses/ece271B-F09/handouts/Dimensionality.pdf, 2009. [ bib ]

[11] Dave Richeson. Volumes of n-dimensional balls, 2010. [ bib | http ]
[12] Lee Gomes. Machine-Learning Maestro Michael Jordan on the Delusions of Big Data and Other Huge Engineering Efforts. [13] Nate Silver. The signal and the noise: Why so many predictions fail-but some don't. Penguin, 2012. [ bib ]
[14] Ben Goldacre. Bad science: Quacks, hacks, and big pharma flacks. Random House LLC, 2010. [ bib ]
[15] Leonard Mlodinow. The drunkard's walk: How randomness rules our lives. Random House LLC, 2008. [ bib ]
[16] Christian Böhm, Stefan Berchtold, and Daniel A Keim. Searching in high-dimensional spaces: Index structures for improving the performance of multimedia databases. ACM Computing Surveys (CSUR), 33(3):322-373, 2001. [ bib ]

[19] Edwin Abbott. Flatland. Broadview Press, 2009. [ bib ]
